nodes.context: Powerful criminal organization based in Moscow, active late 1980s to early 2000s.
Russian criminal organization that merged with Orekhovskaya gang in 1989 and later absorbed its remnants.
Criminal gangs from Chechnya that were a threat to Orekhovskaya and Solntsevskaya.
Rival gang to Orekhovskaya according to infobox.
Rival gang to Orekhovskaya according to infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1989 the gang merged with the Solntsevskaya bratva to combat the growing threat of Chechen mafia gangs.
edges.context: Orekhovskaya merged with Solntsevskaya bratva in 1989 to fight Chechen mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: However, by 1997 the alliance had split and Todorov was again running an independent gang.
edges.context: The alliance between Orekhovskaya and Solntsevskaya ended by 1997.

edges.evidence_quote: what was left of the gang was swallowed up by the Solntsevskaya Bratva
edges.context: Remnants of Orekhovskaya were absorbed by Solntsevskaya Bratva after the split.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1989 the gang merged with the Solntsevskaya bratva to combat the growing threat of Chechen mafia gangs.
edges.context: Orekhovskaya and Solntsevskaya allied against Chechen mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Kuntsevskaya gang, Caucasian gangs, Izmailovskaya gang
edges.context: Kuntsevskaya gang is listed as a rival in the infobox.
